title: Infusion management system
abstract: A method includes receiving, by a master infusion device from a server, at least one fluid delivery protocol. The fluid delivery protocol includes one or more parameters for delivering a fluid to a user. The method also includes configuring, by the master infusion device, a first slave infusion pump with a first fluid delivery protocol of the at least one fluid delivery protocol. The method further includes configuring, by the master infusion device, a second slave infusion pump with a second fluid delivery protocol of the at least one fluid delivery protocol. The method also includes delivering, by the first slave infusion pump and based on the first fluid delivery protocol, the fluid to the user. Related methods and articles of manufacture, including apparatuses and computer program products, are also disclosed.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A system, comprising: at least one data processor; and at least one memory storing instructions which, when executed by the at least one data processor, result in operations comprising: receiving, by a master infusion device from a server, at least one fluid delivery protocol, the fluid delivery protocol comprising one or more parameters for delivering a fluid to a user; configuring, by the master infusion device, a first slave infusion pump with a first fluid delivery protocol of the at least one fluid delivery protocol; configuring, by the master infusion device, a second slave infusion pump with a second fluid delivery protocol of the at least one fluid delivery protocol; and delivering, by the first slave infusion pump and based on the first fluid delivery protocol, the fluid to the user. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the first slave infusion pump is communicatively coupled with a user device, the user device configured to display at least one of the one or more parameters for delivering the fluid to the user. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the first slave infusion pump does not comprise a display. 4. The system of any of claims 1 to 3, wherein the operations further comprise: measuring, by the first slave infusion pump, one or more fluid delivery parameters associated with the delivery of the fluid; transmitting, by the first slave infusion pump to a user device in wireless communication with the first slave infusion pump, the one or more fluid delivery parameters; and transmitting, by the user device to the server, the one or more fluid delivery parameters. 5. The system of any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the receiving further comprises authenticating a clinician to provide access to the fluid delivery protocol on the server. 6. The system of any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the configuring the first slave infusion pump comprises establishing a secure connection between the first slave infusion pump and the master infusion device, the establishing comprising encrypting transmissions between the first slave infusion pump and the master infusion device. 7. The system of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the first fluid delivery protocol is different from the second fluid delivery protocol, wherein the first slave infusion pump is configured to deliver the fluid to a first user based on the first fluid delivery protocol, and wherein the second slave infusion pump is configured to deliver the fluid to a second user based on the second fluid delivery protocol. 8. The system of any of claims 1 to 7, wherein the operations further comprise: detecting an error in the delivery of the fluid to the user; and stopping the delivery of the fluid to the user. 9. The system of claim 8, wherein the operations further comprise: transmitting the error to a user device in wireless communication with the first slave infusion pump; and displaying information associated with the error via the user device. 10. A method, comprising: receiving, by a master infusion device from a server, at least one fluid delivery protocol, the fluid delivery protocol comprising one or more parameters for delivering a fluid to a user; configuring, by the master infusion device, a first slave infusion pump with a first fluid delivery protocol of the at least one fluid delivery protocol; configuring, by the master infusion device, a second slave infusion pump with a second fluid delivery protocol of the at least one fluid delivery protocol; and delivering, by the first slave infusion pump and based on the first fluid delivery protocol, the fluid to the user. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the first slave infusion pump is communicatively coupled with a user device, the user device configured to display at least one of the one or more parameters for delivering the fluid to the user. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the first slave infusion pump does not comprise a display. 13. The method of any of claims 10 to 12, further comprising: measuring, by the first slave infusion pump, one or more fluid delivery parameters associated with the delivery of the fluid; transmitting, by the first slave infusion pump to a user device in wireless communication with the first slave infusion pump, the one or more fluid delivery parameters; and transmitting, by the user device to the server, the one or more fluid delivery parameters. 14. The method of any of claims 10 to 13, wherein the receiving further comprises authenticating a clinician to provide access to the fluid delivery protocol on the server. 15. The method of any of claims 10 to 14, wherein the configuring the first slave infusion pump comprises establishing a secure connection between the first slave infusion pump and the master infusion device, the establishing comprising encrypting transmissions between the first slave infusion pump and the master infusion device. 16. The method of any of claims 10 to 15, wherein the first fluid delivery protocol is different from the second fluid delivery protocol, wherein the first slave infusion pump is configured to deliver the fluid to a first user based on the first fluid delivery protocol, and wherein the second slave infusion pump is configured to deliver the fluid to a second user based on the second fluid delivery protocol. 17. The method of any of claims 10 to 16, further comprising: detecting an error in the delivery of the fluid to the user; and stopping the delivery of the fluid to the user. 18. The method of any of claim 17, further comprising: transmitting the error to a user device in wireless communication with the first slave infusion pump; and displaying information associated with the error via the user device. 19. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium including program code, which when executed by at least one data processor, cause operations comprising: receiving, by a master infusion device from a server, at least one fluid delivery protocol, the fluid delivery protocol comprising one or more parameters for delivering a fluid to a user; 

 configuring, by the master infusion device, a first slave infusion pump with a first fluid delivery protocol of the at least one fluid delivery protocol; configuring, by the master infusion device, a second slave infusion pump with a second fluid delivery protocol of the at least one fluid delivery protocol; and delivering, by the first slave infusion pump and based on the first fluid delivery protocol, the fluid to the user. 20. An apparatus, comprising: means for receiving, by a master infusion device from a server, at least one fluid delivery protocol, the fluid delivery protocol comprising one or more parameters for delivering a fluid to a user; means for configuring, by the master infusion device, a first slave infusion pump with a first fluid delivery protocol of the at least one fluid delivery protocol; means for configuring, by the master infusion device, a second slave infusion pump with a second fluid delivery protocol of the at least one fluid delivery protocol; and means for delivering, by the first slave infusion pump and based on the first fluid delivery protocol, the fluid to the user. 21. The apparatus of claim 20, comprising: means for performing any of the functions recited in any of claims 11 to 18.
